ORIGINAL ARTICLE
Performance assessment of the BDS‑3 PPP‑B2b service for real‑time 
earthquake source description: a case study for the 2021 Mw 7.4 
Maduo earthquake
Jianfei Zang1 · Shijie Fan1 · Caijun Xu2 · Zhicai Li3 · Rongxin Fang4 · Yidong Lou4
Received: 13 August 2023 / Accepted: 24 October 2023 
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature 2023
Abstract
Retrieving the coseismic displacement in real time is essential when the high-rate Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) 
is used for the real-time earthquake source description.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: BDS-3 initiated a real-time precise point positioning service through the B2b signal (PPP-B2b)

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
2021), where precise satellite orbit and clock corrections are 
broadcast to users through the B2b signal of BDS-3 geosta-
tionary earth orbit (GEO) satellites.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: precise satellite orbit and clock corrections are broadcast to users through the B2b signal of BDS-3 geostationary earth orbit (GEO) satellites

experiment_epoch.description: Case study for a single earthquake event. GNSS observations collected during the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo earthquake.

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
In this study, the performance of the PPP-B2b on the real-time 
retrieval of coseismic displacement was first validated with GNSS observations collected during the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo 
earthquake.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: performance of the PPP-B2b on the real-time retrieval of coseismic displacement was first validated with GNSS observations collected during the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo earthquake

dcb_handling.note: Earthquake monitoring application paper — focuses on PPP displacement retrieval, not correction composition details

mathematical_model.processing_mode: Real-time PPP with dual-frequency ionosphere-free combination
key_methods: PPP-B2b orbit correction recovery: precise orbit = broadcast orbit + correction vector in ECEF
Earthquake magnitude scaling law: log(PGD) = A + B*M_W + C*log(R)
Coseismic displacement retrieval from PPP-B2b time series
Focal mechanism inversion from displacement field

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
Methodology
In this section, the recovery procedure of the precise satel-
lite orbit and clock offsets with the PPP-B2b corrections 
are first introduced.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: PPP-B2b orbit correction recovery: precise orbit = broadcast orbit + correction vector in ECEF

datasets.observation_data: 1-Hz GNSS observations (GPS+BDS) from regional continuous stations around Maduo earthquake epicenter
datasets.correction_source: BDS-3 PPP-B2b real-time orbit and clock corrections broadcast via GEO satellite

Maduo earthquake
Jianfei Zang1 · Shijie Fan1 · Caijun Xu2 · Zhicai Li3 · Rongxin Fang4 · Yidong Lou4
Received: 13 August 2023 / Accepted: 24 October 2023 
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature 2023
Abstract
Retrieving the coseismic displacement in real time is essential when the high-rate Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) 
is used for the real-time earthquake source description.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: GNSS observations collected during the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo earthquake

metrics.earthquake_source_inversion: Warning magnitude, focal mechanism, and fault slip distribution all in good agreement with post-processed PPP results

main_results.summary: PPP-B2b service can provide near-real-time coseismic displacement waveforms for earthquake source description without internet dependency. Combined GPS/BDS-3 PPP-B2b achieves best accuracy (0.3-0.4cm horizontal).
key_findings: Coseismic displacement accuracy: 0.4cm (N), 0.3cm (E), 0.4cm (U) for combined GPS/BDS-3 PPP-B2b
Warning magnitude from PPP-B2b consistent with catalog values
Focal mechanism inversion results consistent with reference (USGS, CENC)
PPP-B2b enables internet-free real-time earthquake monitoring globally

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
Our results indicate that the accuracies of the coseismic displacements in 
the north, east and up components are 0.8 cm, 0.5 cm, and 1.4 cm for the single GPS PPP-B2b, 0.5 cm, 0.6 cm, and 0.9 cm 
for the single BDS-3 PPP-B2b and 0.4 cm, 0.3 cm, and 0.4 cm for the combined GPS/BDS-3 PPP-B2b, respectively, when 
compared with the coseismic displacements of the post-processing precise point positioning (PPP).
grounding_quotes.original_quote: accuracies of the coseismic displacements in the north, east and up components are 0.8 cm, 0.5 cm, and 1.4 cm for the single GPS PPP-B2b

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
Moreover, the warning 
magnitude, focal mechanism and fault slip distribution inverted with the PPP-B2b displacements are all in good agreement 
with those inverted with the PPP displacements.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: the warning magnitude, focal mechanism and fault slip distribution inverted with the PPP-B2b displacements are all in good agreement with those inverted with the PPP displacements

novelty_audit.rationale: First assessment of BDS-3 PPP-B2b service for real-time earthquake source description — a novel application combining GNSS seismology with satellite-based PPP. Provides a globally available, internet-free alternative to terrestrial RTS for earthquake early warning.

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
This study highlights the great possibility of the PPP-B2b to be used for 
earthquake early warning and rapid emergency responses.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: This study highlights the great possibility of the PPP-B2b to be used for earthquake early warning and rapid emergency responses

reproduction_blockers: GNSS observation data from Maduo earthquake not publicly archived
PPP-B2b correction stream for the event period not publicly archived
No processing configuration details for PPP displacement retrieval

bibliographic_info.title: Performance assessment of the BDS-3 PPP-B2b service for real-time earthquake source description: a case study for the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo earthquake
